item.title: F-037: Voice Control — Intent-Based Editing
item.intent: Users should be able to speak what they want their document to become — not dictate content, but describe changes. AI interprets the intent and executes. This is hands-free, AI-native document editing.
item.description: Hold mic button (or Cmd+Shift+J) to activate. User speaks an editing intent in natural language. AI interprets and maps to an operation: rewrite for conciseness, add a section, change tone, restructure, generate content at cursor, analyze the document. Shows same inline diff preview as touch/keyboard AI — user accepts, edits, or dismisses. Uses iOS Speech framework (on-device recognition, no network required on iOS 17+). Context-aware: operates on current selection or cursor position. Voice never modifies without confirmation. Depends on: FEAT-041 (AI Model Pipeline), FEAT-054 (AI Action Bar).
acceptanceCriteria: User holds mic button, says "make this paragraph shorter", releases — AI shows inline diff within 3 seconds
Speech recognition begins within 200ms of activation
Transcription shown in real-time as user speaks
Voice commands work on current selection (if selected) or current paragraph
All voice-initiated changes show inline diff preview — never modifies without confirmation
Works offline using on-device speech recognition (iOS 17+)
Cmd+Shift+J keyboard shortcut activates voice on iPad with external keyboard
VoiceOver users can distinguish between VoiceOver navigation and voice editing commands
Chained voice commands (speak, accept, speak again) work fluidly without re-activating
